description: A blog feature allowing users to view, create, and manage blog posts with a NestJS backend API and React frontend UI

tasks.task:
Create the Blog module with CRUD API endpoints. Following the NestJS patterns in .claude/skills/development.md:

1. Create src/modules/blog/ directory
2. Create blog.dto.ts with CreateBlogPostDto and UpdateBlogPostDto interfaces (title: string, content: string, author: string)
3. Create blog.service.ts with in-memory storage and methods: findAll(), findOne(id), create(dto), update(id, dto), delete(id)
4. Create blog.controller.ts with routes: GET /blog, GET /blog/:id, POST /blog, PATCH /blog/:id, DELETE /blog/:id
5. Create blog.module.ts registering the controller and service
6. Import BlogModule in app.module.ts
7. Enable CORS in main.ts for frontend access: app.enableCors()

Test endpoints with curl after implementation.

Create Blog UI components to display and manage blog posts. Following the React patterns in .claude/skills/development.md:

1. Create src/components/ directory
2. Create src/components/BlogList.tsx - component to fetch and display list of blog posts from http://localhost:3000/blog
3. Create src/components/BlogPost.tsx - component to display a single blog post with title, content, author, and date
4. Create src/components/BlogForm.tsx - form component to create new posts with title, content, author fields
5. Update src/App.tsx to include the blog components with state management for selected post and form visibility
6. Add blog-specific styles to src/style.css for cards, forms, and layout

Use fetch API for HTTP requests to backend. Use useState and useEffect hooks for state and data fetching.

example_backend: POST /blog creates a new blog post and returns it with generated id
GET /blog returns array with the created post
example_frontend: Blog list displays 'No posts yet' when empty
Create post form appears when 'New Post' button is clicked
Submitting the form creates a post and displays it in the list
Clicking a post shows its full content
Posts display title, author, and truncated content in list view
Delete button removes the post from the list
